  // Helper function to read a cookie value
  function getCookie(name) {
    var value = "; " + document.cookie;
    var parts = value.split("; " + name + "=");
    if (parts.length == 2) return parts.pop().split(";").shift();
  }

  // Helper function to set a cookie
  function setCookie(name, value, days) {
    var expires = "";
    if (days) {
      var date = new Date();
      date.setTime(date.getTime() + (days*24*60*60*1000));
      expires = "; expires=" + date.toUTCString();
    }
    document.cookie = name + "=" + (value || "")  + expires + "; path=/";
  }

  // Function to update display eligibility based on the time gap
  function updateDisplayEligibility(waitTimeSeconds) {
    setTimeout(function() {
      window.optinMonsterDisplayEligible = 'true';
      console.log('updateDisplayEligibility - OptinMonster is now display eligible');
    }, waitTimeSeconds * 1000); // Convert seconds to milliseconds
  }

  document.addEventListener('om.Campaign.show', function(event) {
    // console.log('om.Campaign.show', event.detail.Campaign);

    var now = new Date().getTime();
    setCookie('optinMonsterLastDisplayTimestamp', now, 1); // Store timestamp in cookie for 1 day
    window.optinMonsterDisplayEligible = 'false';

    updateDisplayEligibility(minimumDisplayGap);
  });

  var now = new Date().getTime();
  var lastDisplayTime = parseInt(getCookie('optinMonsterLastDisplayTimestamp')) || 0;
  var timePassed = (now - lastDisplayTime) / 1000; // Convert milliseconds to seconds

  if (timePassed >= minimumDisplayGap) {
    window.optinMonsterDisplayEligible = 'true';
    console.log('OptinMonster display eligible on page load');
  } else {
    var waitTimeSeconds = minimumDisplayGap - timePassed;
    window.optinMonsterDisplayEligible = 'false';
    updateDisplayEligibility(waitTimeSeconds);
  }</script> <style id="uagb-style-conditional-extension">@media (min-width: 1025px){body .uag-hide-desktop.uagb-google-map__wrap,body .uag-hide-desktop{display:none !important}}@media (min-width:       }
      
      .container {

      @media (max-width: 768px) {

      }

      @media (max-width: 480px) {

              padding: 8px;

      }
      
      .header {

      }

      .header p {

      }
      
      .form-grid {

      @media (max-width: 768px) {

          }
      }

      @media (max-width: 480px) {

              gap: 15px;
              margin-bottom: 15px;
          }

      .card {

      }
      
      .form-group.visible {

      .results {

      .results.show {

          animation: fadeIn 0.3s ease-in;
      }
      
      @keyframes fadeIn {
          from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(10px); }

      .results-grid {
          display: grid;

          gap: 24px;

      .result-section h3 {

      .bf-slider-group {

      .bf-slider-value {

      .bf-slider {
          width: 100%;
          height: 6px;

          -webkit-appearance: none;

      }

      .bf-slider::-webkit-slider-thumb {

          width: 20px;

      /* Help Modal Styles */
      .help-modal {
          position: fixed;

          left: 0;
          right: 0;

          align-items: center;

          padding: 20px;
          z-index: 1000;
      }

      .help-modal.show {
          display: flex;
      }

      .help-modal-content {
          background: white;
          border-radius: 12px;

      .help-modal-close {

          height: 24px;

      .help-modal-close:hover {

      .help-modal-text {

      /* Timeline Section Styles */
      .timeline-section {

      #goal-specific-note {

When adjusting protein, calories stay constant. When adjusting carbs or fats, protein and calories remain constant.</p></div></div></div></div> <script>// Store original calculated values
      let originalMacros = {
          calories: 0,
          protein: 0,
          fat: 0,
          carbs: 0,
          timeline: ''
      };

      // Current adjusted values
      let currentMacros = {
          calories: 0,
          protein: 0,
          fat: 0,
          carbs: 0,
          timeline: ''
      };

      // Animation state
      let currentStep = 0;
      let animationInterval;

      // Help modal functions
      function showHelpModal(key) {
          let text = '';
          switch(key) {
              case 'target-weight':
                  text = '<b>Target body weight</b> is used to set protein intake when cutting, when body fat percentage is not known. This ensures protein is not set unnecessarily high while still supporting your goals.\nI\'ve explained our reasoning and evidence for this decision in the notes below the calculator, but feel free to use the sliders to adjust it after you have calculated though if you feel strongly about changing it.';
                  break;
              case 'activity-level':
                  text = 'Choose the one that seems to fit you best, and don\'t sweat your choice too much. Calorie calculations are estimations. Our goal here is to get you a starting point that is in the right ballpark. Once you have enough tracking data, I will show you fine-tune it.<br><br><b>Sedentary (<5,000 steps per day):</b> Little to no daily activity. You have a desk job, drive everywhere, and sit most of the day. (x1.25)<br><br><b>Mostly Sedentary (<5,000 steps per day) + strength training:</b> As above, but you lift 3-6 days per week. (x1.45)<br><br><b>Lightly Active (5,000-10,000 steps) + strength training:</b> You lift 3-6 days per week, you\'re on your feet a bit more — light walking or movement during the day, and you might do some other exercise once or twice per week. (x1.65)<br><br><b>Active (10,000-15,000 steps) + strength training:</b> Aside from lifting 3-6 days per week, you\'re physically active most of the day, or do some other exercise on multiple days of the week. (x1.85)<br><br><b>Highly Active (15,000+ steps) + strength training::</b> Aside from lifting 3-6 days per week, you have a very physically active job or participate in intense exercise most days per week. (x2.05)<br><br>Each activity level bump multiplies the result by an additional 0.2.';
                  break;
              case 'primary-goal':
                  text = 'If unsure how to choose your primary goal, see: <b><a href="https://rippedbody.com/cut-or-bulk/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Should I Cut, Bulk, or Recomp?</a></b>';
                  break;
              case 'fat-preference':
                  const currentGoal = document.getElementById('goal').value;
                  if (currentGoal === 'cutting') {
                      text = 'We recommend that 15-25% of your calories come from fat when cutting. The lower range when cutting is because of the relatively higher importance of keeping up carbohydrate intake for training performance to prevent muscle loss.\n<b>Lower side:</b> 15% of calories\n<b>Standard:</b> 20% of calories\n<b>Higher side:</b> 25% of calories';
                  } else if (currentGoal === 'bulking') {
                      text = 'We recommend that 20-30% of your calories come from fat when bulking.\n<b>Lower side:</b> 20% of calories\n<b>Standard:</b> 25% of calories\n<b>Higher side:</b> 30% of calories';
                  } else {
                      text = 'For recomposition, we recommend 20-30% of your calories come from fat.\n<b>Lower side:</b> 20% of calories\n<b>Standard:</b> 25% of calories\n<b>Higher side:</b> 30% of calories';
                  }
                  break;
              default:
                  text = key; // fallback for direct text
          }
          
          document.getElementById('help-modal-text').innerHTML = text.replace(/\n/g, '<br><br>');
          document.getElementById('help-modal').classList.add('show');
      }

      function closeHelpModal() {
          document.getElementById('help-modal').classList.remove('show');
      }

      // Start the progressive reveal animation
      function startProgressiveReveal() {
          // Show first field immediately
          showNextField();
          
          // Then show subsequent fields with delay
          animationInterval = setInterval(() => {
              if (!showNextField()) {
                  clearInterval(animationInterval);
              }
          }, 100); // 100ms delay between each field
      }

      function showNextField() {
          currentStep++;
          const nextField = document.querySelector(`[data-step="${currentStep}"]`);
          
          if (nextField && !nextField.classList.contains('hidden')) {
              nextField.classList.add('visible');
              return true;
          } else if (nextField && nextField.classList.contains('hidden')) {
              // Skip hidden fields and try the next one
              return showNextField();
          }
          
          return false;
      }

      // Update units when changed
      document.getElementById('unit').addEventListener('change', function() {
          const isImperial = this.value === 'imperial';
          document.getElementById('weight-unit').textContent = isImperial ? '(lbs)' : '(kg)';
          document.getElementById('target-weight-unit').textContent = isImperial ? '(lbs)' : '(kg)';
          
          // Update weight values when switching to metric
          if (!isImperial) {
              document.getElementById('weight').value = '90';
              document.getElementById('target-weight').value = '80';
          }
          
          document.getElementById('imperial-height').classList.toggle('hidden', !isImperial);
          document.getElementById('metric-height').classList.toggle('hidden', isImperial);
          
          // Show the appropriate height field if animation has progressed far enough
          if (currentStep >= 5) {
              if (isImperial) {
                  document.querySelector('#imperial-height .form-group[data-step="5"]').classList.add('visible');
                  if (currentStep >= 6) {
                      document.querySelector('#imperial-height .form-group[data-step="6"]').classList.add('visible');
                  }
              } else {
                  document.getElementById('metric-height').classList.add('visible');
              }
          }
          
          // Update rate displays if they're visible
          if (document.getElementById('goal').value === 'cutting') {
              updateCuttingRateValue(document.getElementById('cutting-rate-slider').value);
          } else if (document.getElementById('goal').value === 'bulking') {
              updateBulkingRateValue(document.getElementById('bulking-rate-slider').value);
          }
      });

// Show/hide rate sliders based on goal
document.getElementById('goal').addEventListener('change', function() {
    const selectedGoal = this.value;
    const isBulking = selectedGoal === 'bulking';
    const isCutting = selectedGoal === 'cutting';
    const isRecomp = selectedGoal === 'recomp';
    
    document.getElementById('bulking-rate-group').classList.toggle('hidden', !isBulking);
    document.getElementById('cutting-rate-group').classList.toggle('hidden', !isCutting);
    
    // Hide target weight for bulking and recomposition
    const targetWeightGroup = document.querySelector('[data-step="13"]');
    targetWeightGroup.classList.toggle('hidden', isBulking || isRecomp);
    
    if (isBulking && currentStep >= 14) {
        document.getElementById('bulking-rate-group').classList.add('visible');
    }
    
    if (isCutting && currentStep >= 14) {
        document.getElementById('cutting-rate-group').classList.add('visible');
    }
    
    // Show target weight field if cutting and animation has progressed
    if (isCutting && currentStep >= 13) {
        targetWeightGroup.classList.add('visible');
    }
    
    // Update rate displays
    if (isCutting) {
        updateCuttingRateValue(document.getElementById('cutting-rate-slider').value);
    } else if (isBulking) {
        updateBulkingRateValue(document.getElementById('bulking-rate-slider').value);
    }
});

      // Show/hide training days based on macro cycling
      document.getElementById('macro-cycling').addEventListener('change', function() {
          const isCycling = this.value === 'yes';
          document.getElementById('training-days-group').classList.toggle('hidden', !isCycling);
          
          if (isCycling && currentStep >= 18) {
              document.getElementById('training-days-group').classList.add('visible');
          }
      });

      // Show/hide body fat percentage input
      document.getElementById('know-bodyfat').addEventListener('change', function() {
          const knowsBodyFat = this.value === 'yes';
          document.getElementById('bodyfat-group').classList.toggle('hidden', !knowsBodyFat);
          
          if (knowsBodyFat && currentStep >= 10) {
              document.getElementById('bodyfat-group').classList.add('visible');
          }
      });

      function updateBodyFatValue(value) {
          document.getElementById('bodyfat-value').textContent = value + '%';
      }
      
      function updateCuttingRateValue(value) {
          const percentage = parseFloat(value);
          const weight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('weight').value) || 200;
          const unit = document.getElementById('unit').value;
          const unitText = unit === 'imperial' ? 'lbs' : 'kg';
          
          const weeklyWeightLoss = weight * (percentage / 100);
          
          document.getElementById('cutting-rate-percentage').textContent = percentage + '%';
          document.getElementById('cutting-rate-amount').textContent = weeklyWeightLoss.toFixed(1) + ' ' + unitText + '/week';
      }
      
      function updateBulkingRateValue(value) {
          const percentage = parseFloat(value);
          const weight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('weight').value) || 200;
          const unit = document.getElementById('unit').value;
          const unitText = unit === 'imperial' ? 'lbs' : 'kg';
          
          const monthlyWeightGain = weight * (percentage / 100);
          
          document.getElementById('bulking-rate-percentage').textContent = percentage + '%';
          document.getElementById('bulking-rate-amount').textContent = monthlyWeightGain.toFixed(1) + ' ' + unitText + '/month';
      }

      function calculateBMR() {
          const unit = document.getElementById('unit').value;
          const gender = document.getElementById('gender').value;
          const age = parseInt(document.getElementById('age').value);
          const weight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('weight').value);
          const knowsBodyFat = document.getElementById('know-bodyfat').value === 'yes';
          
          let height;
          if (unit === 'imperial') {
              const feet = parseInt(document.getElementById('height-feet').value) || 0;
              const inches = parseInt(document.getElementById('height-inches').value) || 0;
              height = feet * 12 + inches;
              // Convert to cm for the formula
              height = height * 2.54;
          } else {
              height = parseFloat(document.getElementById('height-cm').value);
          }

          // Convert weight to kg if imperial
          let weightKg = weight;
          if (unit === 'imperial') {
              weightKg = weight / 2.20462;
          }

          let bmr;

          if (knowsBodyFat) {
              // Use body fat percentage formula
              const bodyFatPercent = parseFloat(document.getElementById('bodyfat-slider').value);
              const fatMass = (bodyFatPercent / 100) * weightKg;
              const fatFreeMass = weightKg - fatMass;
              
              // BMR = 50.2 × FFM^0.7 + 40.5 × (FFM^0.7 × FM^0.066) – [1.1;2.75] × Age
              let ageReduction;
              if (age <= 60) {
                  ageReduction = 1.1 * age;
              } else {
                  ageReduction = 1.1 * 60 + 2.75 * (age - 60);
              }
              
              const ffmComponent = 50.2 * Math.pow(fatFreeMass, 0.7);
              const interactionComponent = 40.5 * (Math.pow(fatFreeMass, 0.7) * Math.pow(fatMass, 0.066));
              
              bmr = ffmComponent + interactionComponent - ageReduction;
          } else {
              // Use original formula
              // BMR = 129.6 × Weight^0.55 + 0.011 × Height^2 – [1.96;4.9] × Age – 213.8 × Sex
              const sex = gender === 'male' ? 0 : 1;
              
              let ageReduction;
              if (age <= 60) {
                  ageReduction = 1.96 * age;
              } else {
                  ageReduction = 1.96 * 60 + 4.9 * (age - 60);
              }

              bmr = 129.6 * Math.pow(weightKg, 0.55) + 0.011 * Math.pow(height, 2) - ageReduction - 213.8 * sex;
          }

          // Apply metabolic adaptations
          let bmrReduction = 1.0;
          const inDeficit = document.getElementById('energy-deficit').value === 'yes';
          const isWeightReduced = document.getElementById('weight-reduced').value === 'yes';

          if (inDeficit) {
              bmrReduction *= 0.95; // Reduce BMR by 5%
          }
          if (isWeightReduced) {
              bmrReduction *= 0.97; // Reduce BMR by 3%
          }

          bmr *= bmrReduction;
          return bmr;
      }

      function calculateTDEE(bmr) {
          const activity = document.getElementById('activity').value;
          const multipliers = {
              'sedentary': 1.25,
              'mostly-sedentary': 1.45,
              'lightly-active': 1.65,
              'active': 1.85,
              'highly-active': 2.05
          };
          return bmr * multipliers[activity];
      }

      function calculateTargetCalories(tdee) {
          const goal = document.getElementById('goal').value;
          const weight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('weight').value);
          const unit = document.getElementById('unit').value;

          if (goal === 'cutting') {
              const cuttingRate = parseFloat(document.getElementById('cutting-rate-slider').value) / 100;
              const multiplier = unit === 'imperial' ? 500 : 1100;
              return tdee - (weight * cuttingRate * multiplier);
          } else if (goal === 'bulking') {
              // Calculate surplus based on bulking rate
              const bulkingRate = parseFloat(document.getElementById('bulking-rate-slider').value) / 100;
              
              // Convert to monthly weight gain in pounds
              let monthlyGainLbs;
              if (unit === 'imperial') {
                  monthlyGainLbs = weight * bulkingRate;
              } else {
                  // Convert kg to lbs for the calculation
                  monthlyGainLbs = weight * bulkingRate * 2.2;
              }
              
              // 1 lb per month = 150 kcal per day surplus
              const dailySurplus = monthlyGainLbs * 150;
              
              return tdee + dailySurplus;
          } else {
              return tdee; // recomp
          }
      }

function calculateMacrosBreakdown(targetCalories) {
    const targetWeight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('target-weight').value);
    const currentWeight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('weight').value);
    const unit = document.getElementById('unit').value;
    const fatPreference = document.getElementById('fat-preference').value;
    const goal = document.getElementById('goal').value;
    const knowsBodyFat = document.getElementById('know-bodyfat').value === 'yes';

    // Protein based on goal and weight
    let protein;
    
    if (knowsBodyFat) {
    // Use lean mass-based calculation when body fat is known
    const bodyFatPercent = parseFloat(document.getElementById('bodyfat-slider').value) || 15;
    
    // Calculate lean mass in kg
    let weightKg = currentWeight;
    if (unit === 'imperial') {
        weightKg = currentWeight / 2.20462;
    }
    const leanMassKg = weightKg * (1 - bodyFatPercent / 100);
    
    // Set protein based on goal
    if (goal === 'cutting') {
        // For cutting: 2.5g per kg lean mass
        protein = leanMassKg * 2.5;
    } else {
        // For bulking or recomping: 2.2g per kg lean mass
        protein = leanMassKg * 2.2;
    }
} else {
        // Use original calculation when body fat is unknown
        let proteinMultiplier;
        let weightForProtein;
        
        if (goal === 'cutting') {
            proteinMultiplier = unit === 'imperial' ? 1.0 : 2.2; // 1g per lb or 2.2g per kg
            weightForProtein = targetWeight; // Use target weight for cutting
        } else if (goal === 'bulking') {
            proteinMultiplier = unit === 'imperial' ? 0.73 : 1.6; // 0.73g per lb or 1.6g per kg
            weightForProtein = currentWeight; // Use current weight for bulking
        } else { // recomposition
            proteinMultiplier = unit === 'imperial' ? 0.73 : 1.6; // 0.73g per lb or 1.6g per kg
            weightForProtein = currentWeight; // Use current weight for recomposition
        }
        
        protein = weightForProtein * proteinMultiplier;
    }
    
    const proteinCalories = protein * 4;

    // Fat percentage based on goal and preference
    let fatPercentage;
    if (goal === 'cutting') {
        // 15-25% for cutting
        if (fatPreference === 'lower') fatPercentage = 0.15;
        else if (fatPreference === 'higher') fatPercentage = 0.25;
        else fatPercentage = 0.2; // standard for cutting
    } else if (goal === 'bulking') {
        // 20-30% for bulking
        if (fatPreference === 'lower') fatPercentage = 0.2;
        else if (fatPreference === 'higher') fatPercentage = 0.3;
        else fatPercentage = 0.25; // standard for bulking
    } else {
        // Recomposition: 20-30% fat
        if (fatPreference === 'lower') fatPercentage = 0.2;
        else if (fatPreference === 'higher') fatPercentage = 0.3;
        else fatPercentage = 0.25; // standard for recomp
    }

    const fat = (targetCalories * fatPercentage) / 9;
    const fatCalories = fat * 9;

    // Remaining calories for carbs
    const carbCalories = targetCalories - proteinCalories - fatCalories;
    const carbs = Math.max(0, carbCalories / 4);

    return {
        protein: Math.round(protein),
        fat: Math.round(fat),
        carbs: Math.round(carbs)
    };
}

      
function calculateMacros() {
  const bmr = calculateBMR();
  const tdee = calculateTDEE(bmr);
  const targetCalories = calculateTargetCalories(tdee);
  const isCycling = document.getElementById('macro-cycling').value === 'yes';
  const goal = document.getElementById('goal').value;
  const unit = document.getElementById('unit').value;
  const knowsBodyFat = document.getElementById('know-bodyfat').value === 'yes';

  if (isCycling) {
      const trainingDays = parseInt(document.getElementById('training-days').value);
      const restDays = 7 - trainingDays;
      const weight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('weight').value);

      // Calculate cycling macros
      let protein;

      if (knowsBodyFat) {
    // Use lean mass-based calculation when body fat is known
    const bodyFatPercent = parseFloat(document.getElementById('bodyfat-slider').value) || 15;
    
    // Calculate lean mass in kg
    let weightKg = weight;
    if (unit === 'imperial') {
        weightKg = weight / 2.20462;
    }
    const leanMassKg = weightKg * (1 - bodyFatPercent / 100);
    
    // Set protein based on goal
    if (goal === 'cutting') {
        // For cutting: 2.5g per kg lean mass
        protein = leanMassKg * 2.5;
    } else {
        // For bulking or recomping: 2.2g per kg lean mass
        protein = leanMassKg * 2.2;
    }
} else {
          // Use original calculation when body fat is unknown
          const currentWeight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('weight').value);
          const targetWeight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('target-weight').value);
          let proteinMultiplier;
          let weightForProtein;
          
          if (goal === 'cutting') {
              proteinMultiplier = unit === 'imperial' ? 1.0 : 2.2;
              weightForProtein = targetWeight;
          } else if (goal === 'bulking') {
              proteinMultiplier = unit === 'imperial' ? 0.73 : 1.6;
              weightForProtein = currentWeight;
          } else { // recomposition
              proteinMultiplier = unit === 'imperial' ? 0.73 : 1.6;
              weightForProtein = currentWeight;
          }
          
          protein = weightForProtein * proteinMultiplier;
      }
      
      // ... rest of the cycling calculation remains the same

      // Calorie distribution for cycling
      let trainingDayCalories, restDayCalories;
      if (goal === 'cutting') {
          trainingDayCalories = targetCalories * 1.1;
          restDayCalories = targetCalories * 0.85;
      } else if (goal === 'bulking') {
          trainingDayCalories = targetCalories * 1.15;
          restDayCalories = targetCalories * 0.9;
      } else {
          trainingDayCalories = targetCalories * 1.05;
          restDayCalories = targetCalories * 0.95;
      }

      // Adjust to maintain weekly average
      const totalWeeklyCalories = targetCalories * 7;
      const currentWeeklyCalories = trainingDayCalories * trainingDays + restDayCalories * restDays;
      const adjustment = totalWeeklyCalories / currentWeeklyCalories;
      trainingDayCalories *= adjustment;
      restDayCalories *= adjustment;

      // Calculate macros for each day type
      // Calculate macros for each day type - now respecting fat preference
      const fatPreference = document.getElementById('fat-preference').value;

      // Calculate base fat percentages based on goal and preference
      let baseFatPercentage;
      if (goal === 'cutting') {
          // 15-25% for cutting
          if (fatPreference === 'lower') baseFatPercentage = 0.15;
          else if (fatPreference === 'higher') baseFatPercentage = 0.25;
          else baseFatPercentage = 0.2; // standard for cutting
      } else if (goal === 'bulking') {
          // 20-30% for bulking
          if (fatPreference === 'lower') baseFatPercentage = 0.2;
          else if (fatPreference === 'higher') baseFatPercentage = 0.3;
          else baseFatPercentage = 0.25; // standard for bulking
      } else {
          // Recomposition: 20-30% fat
          if (fatPreference === 'lower') baseFatPercentage = 0.2;
          else if (fatPreference === 'higher') baseFatPercentage = 0.3;
          else baseFatPercentage = 0.25; // standard for recomp
      }

      // Adjust fat percentages for training vs rest days while maintaining user preference
      const trainingDayFatPercentage = Math.max(0.15, baseFatPercentage * 0.8); // Lower fat on training days
      const restDayFatPercentage = Math.min(0.4, baseFatPercentage * 1.2); // Higher fat on rest days

      const trainingDayFat = (trainingDayCalories * trainingDayFatPercentage) / 9;
      const trainingDayCarbs = (trainingDayCalories - protein * 4 - trainingDayFat * 9) / 4;

      const restDayFat = (restDayCalories * restDayFatPercentage) / 9;
      const restDayCarbs = (restDayCalories - protein * 4 - restDayFat * 9) / 4;

      // Store original values for cycling (use weekly averages for sliders)
      const weeklyAvgFat = (trainingDayFat * trainingDays + restDayFat * restDays) / 7;
      const weeklyAvgCarbs = (trainingDayCarbs * trainingDays + restDayCarbs * restDays) / 7;
      
      originalMacros = {
          calories: Math.round(targetCalories),
          protein: Math.round(protein),
          fat: Math.round(weeklyAvgFat),
          carbs: Math.round(weeklyAvgCarbs),
          // Store cycling data
          isCycling: true,
          trainingDays: trainingDays,
          trainingDay: {
              calories: Math.round(trainingDayCalories),
              protein: Math.round(protein),
              fat: Math.round(trainingDayFat),
              carbs: Math.round(trainingDayCarbs)
          },
          restDay: {
              calories: Math.round(restDayCalories),
              protein: Math.round(protein),
              fat: Math.round(restDayFat),
              carbs: Math.round(restDayCarbs)
          }
      };

      // Update results for cycling
      document.getElementById('bmr-result').textContent = Math.round(bmr) + ' cal';
      document.getElementById('tdee-result').textContent = Math.round(tdee) + ' cal';
      document.getElementById('target-calories').textContent = Math.round(targetCalories) + ' cal (weekly avg)';
      
      document.getElementById('protein-result').textContent = Math.round(protein) + 'g (daily)';
      document.getElementById('fat-result').textContent = `T: ${Math.round(trainingDayFat)}g / R: ${Math.round(restDayFat)}g`;
      document.getElementById('carbs-result').textContent = `T: ${Math.round(trainingDayCarbs)}g / R: ${Math.round(restDayCarbs)}g`;

      // Update breakdown section for cycling
      document.getElementById('protein-percent').textContent = `Training: ${Math.round(trainingDayCalories)} cal`;
      document.getElementById('protein-calories').textContent = `P: ${Math.round(protein)}g, C: ${Math.round(trainingDayCarbs)}g, F: ${Math.round(trainingDayFat)}g`;
      
      document.getElementById('fat-percent').textContent = `Rest: ${Math.round(restDayCalories)} cal`;
      document.getElementById('fat-calories').textContent = `P: ${Math.round(protein)}g, C: ${Math.round(restDayCarbs)}g, F: ${Math.round(restDayFat)}g`;
      
      document.getElementById('carbs-percent').textContent = `Weekly Average: ${Math.round(targetCalories)} cal`;
      document.getElementById('carbs-calories').textContent = `${trainingDays} training days, ${restDays} rest days`;

  } else {
      // Original non-cycling calculation
      const macros = calculateMacrosBreakdown(targetCalories);

      originalMacros = {
          calories: Math.round(targetCalories),
          protein: macros.protein,
          fat: macros.fat,
          carbs: macros.carbs,
          isCycling: false
      };

      document.getElementById('bmr-result').textContent = Math.round(bmr) + ' cal';
      document.getElementById('tdee-result').textContent = Math.round(tdee) + ' cal';
      document.getElementById('target-calories').textContent = Math.round(targetCalories) + ' cal';
      
      document.getElementById('protein-result').textContent = macros.protein + 'g';
      document.getElementById('fat-result').textContent = macros.fat + 'g';
      document.getElementById('carbs-result').textContent = macros.carbs + 'g';

      const proteinPercent = Math.round(((macros.protein * 4) / targetCalories) * 100);
      const fatPercent = Math.round(((macros.fat * 9) / targetCalories) * 100);
      const carbsPercent = Math.round(((macros.carbs * 4) / targetCalories) * 100);

      document.getElementById('protein-percent').textContent = `Protein (${proteinPercent}%)`;
      document.getElementById('fat-percent').textContent = `Fat (${fatPercent}%)`;
      document.getElementById('carbs-percent').textContent = `Carbs (${carbsPercent}%)`;

      document.getElementById('protein-calories').textContent = (macros.protein * 4) + ' cal';
      document.getElementById('fat-calories').textContent = (macros.fat * 9) + ' cal';
      document.getElementById('carbs-calories').textContent = (macros.carbs * 4) + ' cal';
  }

  // Calculate estimated timeline
  let timelineText = '';
  let timelineWeeks = 0;

  if (goal === 'cutting') {
      const currentWeight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('weight').value);
      const targetWeight = parseFloat(document.getElementById('target-weight').value);
      const weightToLose = currentWeight - targetWeight;
      
      if (weightToLose > 0) {
          const cuttingRate = parseFloat(document.getElementById('cutting-rate-slider').value) / 100;
          const weeklyWeightLoss = currentWeight * cuttingRate;
          timelineWeeks = Math.ceil(weightToLose / weeklyWeightLoss);
          
          const months = Math.floor(timelineWeeks / 4.33);
          const remainingWeeks = Math.round(timelineWeeks % 4.33);
          
          if (months > 0) {
              timelineText = `Estimated timeline: ${months} month${months > 1 ? 's' : ''}${remainingWeeks > 0 ? ` and ${remainingWeeks} week${remainingWeeks > 1 ? 's' : ''}` : ''}`;
          } else {
              timelineText = `Estimated timeline: ${timelineWeeks} week${timelineWeeks > 1 ? 's' : ''}`;
          }
          timelineText += ` to reach ${targetWeight}${unit === 'imperial' ? 'lbs' : 'kg'}`;
      } else {
          timelineText = 'Target weight is higher than current weight - consider bulking instead';
      }
  } else if (goal === 'recomp') {
      timelineText = 'Recomposition focuses on body composition changes rather than weight changes. Expect gradual improvements over 6-12 months.';
  }

  // Store timeline for display
  originalMacros.timeline = timelineText;
  currentMacros.timeline = timelineText;

  // Show/hide timeline section based on goal
  const timelineSection = document.querySelector('.timeline-section');
  if (goal === 'bulking') {
      timelineSection.style.display = 'none';
  } else {
      timelineSection.style.display = 'block';
  }

  // Set current macros to original
  currentMacros = { ...originalMacros };

  // Set up sliders with appropriate ranges
  const caloriesSlider = document.getElementById('calories-slider');
  caloriesSlider.min = Math.max(1000, originalMacros.calories - 500);
  caloriesSlider.max = originalMacros.calories + 500;

  const proteinSlider = document.getElementById('protein-slider');
  proteinSlider.min = Math.max(50, originalMacros.protein - 50);
  proteinSlider.max = originalMacros.protein + 100;

  const fatSlider = document.getElementById('fat-slider');
  fatSlider.min = Math.max(20, originalMacros.fat - 30);
  fatSlider.max = originalMacros.fat + 50;

  const carbsSlider = document.getElementById('carbs-slider');
  carbsSlider.min = 0;
  carbsSlider.max = originalMacros.carbs + 100;

  updateDisplay();

  const resultsDiv = document.getElementById('results');
  resultsDiv.classList.add('show');
  resultsDiv.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
}

      
function updateDisplay() {
  if (currentMacros.isCycling) {
      // For cycling, show the cycling format
      document.getElementById('protein-result').textContent = currentMacros.protein + 'g (daily)';
      document.getElementById('fat-result').textContent = `T: ${currentMacros.trainingDay.fat}g / R: ${currentMacros.restDay.fat}g`;
      document.getElementById('carbs-result').textContent = `T: ${currentMacros.trainingDay.carbs}g / R: ${currentMacros.restDay.carbs}g`;
      document.getElementById('target-calories').textContent = Math.round(currentMacros.calories) + ' cal (weekly avg)';

      // Update breakdown section for cycling
      document.getElementById('protein-percent').textContent = `Training: ${currentMacros.trainingDay.calories} cal`;
      document.getElementById('protein-calories').textContent = `P: ${currentMacros.protein}g, C: ${currentMacros.trainingDay.carbs}g, F: ${currentMacros.trainingDay.fat}g`;
      
      document.getElementById('fat-percent').textContent = `Rest: ${currentMacros.restDay.calories} cal`;
      document.getElementById('fat-calories').textContent = `P: ${currentMacros.protein}g, C: ${currentMacros.restDay.carbs}g, F: ${currentMacros.restDay.fat}g`;
      
      document.getElementById('carbs-percent').textContent = `Weekly Average: ${Math.round(currentMacros.calories)} cal`;
      document.getElementById('carbs-calories').textContent = `${currentMacros.trainingDays} training days, ${7 - currentMacros.trainingDays} rest days`;
  } else {
      // Regular non-cycling display
      document.getElementById('protein-result').textContent = currentMacros.protein + 'g';
      document.getElementById('fat-result').textContent = currentMacros.fat + 'g';
      document.getElementById('carbs-result').textContent = currentMacros.carbs + 'g';
      document.getElementById('target-calories').textContent = Math.round(currentMacros.calories) + ' cal';

      // Calculate percentages
      const proteinPercent = Math.round(((currentMacros.protein * 4) / currentMacros.calories) * 100);
      const fatPercent = Math.round(((currentMacros.fat * 9) / currentMacros.calories) * 100);
      const carbsPercent = Math.round(((currentMacros.carbs * 4) / currentMacros.calories) * 100);

      document.getElementById('protein-percent').textContent = `Protein (${proteinPercent}%)`;
      document.getElementById('fat-percent').textContent = `Fat (${fatPercent}%)`;
      document.getElementById('carbs-percent').textContent = `Carbs (${carbsPercent}%)`;

      document.getElementById('protein-calories').textContent = (currentMacros.protein * 4) + ' cal';
      document.getElementById('fat-calories').textContent = (currentMacros.fat * 9) + ' cal';
      document.getElementById('carbs-calories').textContent = (currentMacros.carbs * 4) + ' cal';
  }

  // Update slider values (these work the same for both modes)
  document.getElementById('calories-slider-value').textContent = Math.round(currentMacros.calories) + ' cal';
  document.getElementById('protein-slider-value').textContent = currentMacros.protein + 'g';
  document.getElementById('fat-slider-value').textContent = currentMacros.fat + 'g';
  document.getElementById('carbs-slider-value').textContent = currentMacros.carbs + 'g';

  // Update slider positions
  document.getElementById('calories-slider').value = Math.round(currentMacros.calories);
  document.getElementById('protein-slider').value = currentMacros.protein;
  document.getElementById('fat-slider').value = currentMacros.fat;
  document.getElementById('carbs-slider').value = currentMacros.carbs;

  // Update timeline display
  document.getElementById('timeline-text').textContent = currentMacros.timeline || '';
}

function adjustProtein(newProtein) {
  newProtein = parseInt(newProtein);
  const proteinCalorieDiff = (newProtein - currentMacros.protein) * 4;

  // Keep calories constant, adjust carbs and fat in 2:1 ratio
  currentMacros.protein = newProtein;

  // Distribute calorie difference between carbs and fat in 2:1 ratio (carbs:fat)
  const carbCalorieChange = (proteinCalorieDiff * 2) / 3;
  const fatCalorieChange = (proteinCalorieDiff * 1) / 3;

  currentMacros.carbs = Math.max(0, Math.round(currentMacros.carbs - (carbCalorieChange / 4)));
  currentMacros.fat = Math.max(5, Math.round(currentMacros.fat - (fatCalorieChange / 9)));

  // If cycling, recalculate training and rest day macros
  if (currentMacros.isCycling) {
      recalculateCyclingMacros();
  }

  updateDisplay();
}

function recalculateCyclingMacros() {
  if (!currentMacros.isCycling) return;

  const goal = document.getElementById('goal').value;
  const trainingDays = currentMacros.trainingDays;
  const restDays = 7 - trainingDays;

  // Calculate new training and rest day calories based on current weekly average
  let trainingDayCalories, restDayCalories;
  if (goal === 'cutting') {
      trainingDayCalories = currentMacros.calories * 1.1;
      restDayCalories = currentMacros.calories * 0.85;
  } else if (goal === 'bulking') {
      trainingDayCalories = currentMacros.calories * 1.15;
      restDayCalories = currentMacros.calories * 0.9;
  } else {
      trainingDayCalories = currentMacros.calories * 1.05;
      restDayCalories = currentMacros.calories * 0.95;
  }

  // Adjust to maintain weekly average
  const totalWeeklyCalories = currentMacros.calories * 7;
  const currentWeeklyCalories = trainingDayCalories * trainingDays + restDayCalories * restDays;
  const adjustment = totalWeeklyCalories / currentWeeklyCalories;
  trainingDayCalories *= adjustment;
  restDayCalories *= adjustment;

  // Use current weekly macro averages to distribute to training/rest days
  const proteinCalories = currentMacros.protein * 4;
  const weeklyFatCalories = currentMacros.fat * 9;
  const weeklyCarbCalories = currentMacros.carbs * 4;

  // Calculate the ratio of fat calories to total non-protein calories
  const nonProteinCalories = weeklyFatCalories + weeklyCarbCalories;
  const fatRatio = nonProteinCalories > 0 ? weeklyFatCalories / nonProteinCalories : 0.25;

  // Distribute non-protein calories for each day type while maintaining the user's fat/carb ratio
  const trainingDayNonProteinCalories = trainingDayCalories - proteinCalories;
  const restDayNonProteinCalories = restDayCalories - proteinCalories;

  // For training days: slightly lower fat ratio (multiply by 0.8)
  // For rest days: slightly higher fat ratio (multiply by 1.2)
  const trainingDayFatRatio = Math.min(0.4, fatRatio * 0.8); // Cap at 40%
  const restDayFatRatio = Math.min(0.5, fatRatio * 1.2); // Cap at 50%

  const trainingDayFatCalories = trainingDayNonProteinCalories * trainingDayFatRatio;
  const trainingDayFat = trainingDayFatCalories / 9;
  const trainingDayCarbs = (trainingDayNonProteinCalories - trainingDayFatCalories) / 4;

  const restDayFatCalories = restDayNonProteinCalories * restDayFatRatio;
  const restDayFat = restDayFatCalories / 9;
  const restDayCarbs = (restDayNonProteinCalories - restDayFatCalories) / 4;

  // Update cycling data
  currentMacros.trainingDay = {
      calories: Math.round(trainingDayCalories),
      protein: currentMacros.protein,
      fat: Math.round(trainingDayFat),
      carbs: Math.max(0, Math.round(trainingDayCarbs))
  };

  currentMacros.restDay = {
      calories: Math.round(restDayCalories),
      protein: currentMacros.protein,
      fat: Math.round(restDayFat),
      carbs: Math.max(0, Math.round(restDayCarbs))
  };
}

function adjustCalories(newCalories) {
  newCalories = parseInt(newCalories);
  const calorieDiff = newCalories - currentMacros.calories;

  // Protein stays the same
  // Distribute calorie difference between carbs and fat in 2:1 ratio (carbs:fat)
  const carbCalorieChange = (calorieDiff * 2) / 3;
  const fatCalorieChange = (calorieDiff * 1) / 3;

  currentMacros.calories = newCalories;
  currentMacros.carbs = Math.max(0, Math.round(currentMacros.carbs + (carbCalorieChange / 4)));
  currentMacros.fat = Math.max(5, Math.round(currentMacros.fat + (fatCalorieChange / 9)));

  // If cycling, recalculate training and rest day macros
  if (currentMacros.isCycling) {
      recalculateCyclingMacros();
  }

  updateDisplay();
}

function adjustFat(newFat) {
  newFat = parseInt(newFat);
  const fatCalorieDiff = (newFat - currentMacros.fat) * 9;

  // Hold calories and protein constant, adjust carbs only
  currentMacros.fat = newFat;
  currentMacros.carbs = Math.max(0, Math.round(currentMacros.carbs - (fatCalorieDiff / 4)));

  // If cycling, recalculate training and rest day macros
  if (currentMacros.isCycling) {
      recalculateCyclingMacros();
  }

  updateDisplay();
}

function adjustCarbs(newCarbs) {
  newCarbs = parseInt(newCarbs);
  const carbCalorieDiff = (newCarbs - currentMacros.carbs) * 4;

  // Hold calories and protein constant, adjust fat only
  currentMacros.carbs = newCarbs;
  currentMacros.fat = Math.max(5, Math.round(currentMacros.fat - (carbCalorieDiff / 9)));

  // If cycling, recalculate training and rest day macros
  if (currentMacros.isCycling) {
      recalculateCyclingMacros();
  }

  updateDisplay();
}

function resetToOriginal() {
  currentMacros = { ...originalMacros };
  updateDisplay();
}

data-mode="shadow" data-lazyload="false" style="display: block; width: 100%;" data-built="true" data-session="9f7241fd-c415-4fd0-ac76-e21af21cb18e"></div><div style="height:40px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>1. <em>“Does This Calculator Work? why should I trust It?”</em></strong></h3><p>Well, you’re right to question what you find on the internet, especially in the <a data-wpil-monitor-id="2" href="https://rippedbody.com/charlatans-fitness-industry/">charlatan-filled fitness industry</a>. I can’t make you trust me, but I can explain how it’s built so that you can make your own informed decision.</p><p>The calculator utilizes a combination of Greg Nuckols’ <a href="https://macrofactorapp.com/macrofactors-bmr/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">MacroFactor BMR formulas</a> and the activity multiplier and macro setting recommendations from my internationally bestselling book, <em><a aria-label=" (opens in a new tab)" href="https://muscleandstrengthpyramids.com/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">The Muscle and Strength Pyramid: Nutrition</a></em>. The former is one of the most popular nutrition loggers in the world, created by my dear friends over at <a href="https://www.strongerbyscience.com/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">StrongerbyScience</a>. The latter is a fully referenced 300-page book that I co-authored with <a data-wpil-monitor-id="3" href="https://rippedbody.com/podcast-eric-helms/">Dr. Eric Helms</a> and Andrea Valdez. (It’s popular with physique competitors, powerlifters, and coaches. The trainers in your gym have probably heard of it.)</p><p>We’ve been <a data-wpil-monitor-id="4" href="https://rippedbody.com/coaching/">coaching online</a> as a full-time job since 2011. If we didn’t get results for our clients (<a href="https://rippedbody.com/results/">view hundreds of our client results photos here</a>), we wouldn’t still be in business.</p><h4 class="wp-block-heading">How BMR Is Calculated</h4><p>The <strong><em>basic details</em> section</strong> is used to calculate your basal metabolic rate (BMR). Think of your BMR as your ‘coma calories’ — the energy intake you need, should you fall into a coma, to maintain your body weight. There are a variety of formulas to calculate BMR.</p><ul class="wp-block-list"><li><strong>Selecting that you’re in an energy deficit</strong> reduces calculated BMR by 5%. This is to take into account metabolic adaptation, not metabolic damage, and is a natural response to a calorie deficit.</li><li><strong>Selecting that you’re currently more than 10% below your all-time highest body weight</strong> reduces calculated BMR by 3%.</li><li><strong>If you enter an estimate of your body fat percentage</strong>, the calculator switches to a different set of equations, which will produce a slightly better estimate for those who are considerably higher or lower than average.</li></ul><h4 class="wp-block-heading">How Activity Multipliers Work</h4><p>An <strong>activity multiplier</strong> in a macro calculator is a number used to estimate your <strong>Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE)</strong> and is based on how active you are. It adjusts your <strong>Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR)</strong> — the calories you’d burn at rest — to account for daily movement and exercise.</p><p>Each activity level bump multiplies the BMR calculation by an additional 0.2. Choose the one that seems to fit you best, and don’t sweat your choice too much. — Calorie calculations are estimations. Our goal here is to get you a starting point that is in the right ballpark. Once you have enough tracking data, I will show you how to fine-tune it.</p><ul class="wp-block-list"><li><strong>Sedentary (<5,000 steps per day):</strong> Little to no daily activity. You have a desk job, drive everywhere, and sit most of the day. (x1.25)</li><li><strong>Mostly Sedentary (<5,000 steps per day) + strength training:</strong> As above, but you lift 3-6 days per week. (x1.45)</li><li><strong>Lightly Active (5,000-10,000 steps) + strength training:</strong> You lift 3-6 days per week, you’re on your feet a bit more — light walking or movement during the day, and you might do some other exercise once or twice per week. (x1.65)</li><li><strong>Active (10,000-15,000 steps) + strength training:</strong> Aside from lifting 3-6 days per week, you’re physically active most of the day, or do some other exercise on multiple days of the week. (x1.85)</li><li><strong>Highly Active (15,000+ steps) + strength training::</strong> Aside from lifting 3-6 days per week, you have a very physically active job or participate in intense exercise most days per week. (x2.05)</li></ul><p>I’ll explain how the macros (protein, carbs, and fat) are calculated in a moment. But first, you’re probably wondering whether your calculations are ‘correct’ for you. It’s natural to wonder that, but this shows a fundamental misunderstanding of how calculators work. That’s because…</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>2. You <em>will</em> need to adjust these macros sooner or later to achieve the desired rate of weight change</strong></h3><div style="height:20px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div><figure class="extendedwopts-md-center wp-block-embed aligncenter is-type-video is-provider-vimeo wp-block-embed-vimeo wp-embed-aspect-16-9 wp-has-aspect-ratio"><div class="wp-block-embed__wrapper"> <iframe src="https://player.vimeo.com/video/976212662?dnt=1&app_id=122963" width="768" height="432" frameborder="0" allow="autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write"></iframe></div><figcaption class="wp-element-caption"><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👆" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f446.svg"> <strong>How People Use a Macro Calculator Incorrectly</strong> <img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👆" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f446.svg"></figcaption></figure><div style="height:64px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div><p><strong><em>Why sooner?</em> </strong>Because the calculations are based on equations derived from group averages. You might be on either side of this average, so consider them a starting point from which to adjust.</p><p><strong><em>Why later?</em> </strong>Because energy needs change over time as we diet and bulk. Your <a href="http://- [x] https://rippedbody.com/finding-calorie-maintenance/#metabolic-adaptation">metabolism will gradually adapt to fight a caloric deficit</a>, and energy needs increase when we gain weight. These things happen for some people more than others, and this is not something a calculator can predict.</p><p>For these two reasons, <a href="https://rippedbody.com/dont-recalculate/">I don’t recommend using a calculator when transitioning between cutting, bulking, and maintenance</a>. Use your progress data instead:</p><p><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👉" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f449.svg"> <a href="https://rippedbody.com/how-to-cut-after-bulking/">How To Cut After Bulking</a><br><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👉" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f449.svg"> <a href="https://rippedbody.com/finding-calorie-maintenance/" type="post" id="36799">How To Find Maintenance After Dieting (or Bulking)</a><br><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👉" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f449.svg"> <a href="https://rippedbody.com/how-to-bulk-after-cutting/">How To Bulk After Cutting</a></p><p>Knowing how impatient people are to see results, we can factor in the metabolic adaptation into calorie and macro calculations.</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity is-style-wide"><h4 class="wp-block-heading">But before we get into that…</h4><p>People consistently make the same simple mistakes when acting on these calculations. So, I have built a <strong>Free 15-Day Transformation Kickstart Bundle</strong> that has helped 100,000 people so far avoid them. May I send that to you?</p><p><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👉" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f449.svg"> <strong>Training & nutrition plan</strong> to !important;}</style></form></div><p class="has-text-align-center has-small-font-size"><strong>Your email won’t be shared, sold, or abused – ever.</strong></p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity is-style-wide"><p>So, as I was saying, I’ve factored the energy need adaptions into the calculations in the following couple of ways…</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>3. For those Who Have Already Been Dieting…</strong></h3><p>You’ll see two questions after you enter your height.</p><p><strong><em>Are you currently in an energy deficit (i.e., are you currently losing weight)? </em></strong><br>Being in an energy deficit tends to slightly decrease BMR. This is called metabolic adaptation, not metabolic damage, and is a natural response to a calorie deficit. Selecting this reduces calculated BMR by 5%.</p><p><strong><em>Are you currently more than 10% below your all-time highest body weight?</em></strong><br>Being in a weight-reduced state tends to slightly decrease BMR. Selecting this reduces calculated BMR by 3%.</p><p>Additionally, the total nerds among you (I love you guys!) who have been pulling out your calculators to check my math may have noticed that…</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>4. For those bulking, I’ve upped the caloric surplus by 50% to anticipate some of the increased energy needs when bulking</strong></h3><p>This is not an extreme change; we’re talking a 50 calorie bump per pound you are looking to gain each month.</p><p>The reason I did this is that there’s a tricky little bitch called NEAT, which can impact energy needs <em>way</em> more from person to person, especially when bulking.</p><p>NEAT is the nickname for ‘non-exercise activity thermogenesis,’ which is the energy expended for everything we do aside from sleeping, digesting food, or sports-like exercise. It ranges from the energy expended walking to work, typing and texting friends, shaking up a protein shake, performing yard work, and fidgeting.</p><p>Some people ramp up NEAT much more than others, which explains those who claim not to be able to gain weight. These people are commonly referred to as ‘hard gainers,’ but there is nothing wrong with them; they simply need to eat more.</p><p>This cannot be factored into the macro calculator, which is why <a href="https://rippedbody.com/diet-progress-tracking/">tracking average weight changes</a> and adjusting caloric intake based on the outcome is so critical.</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>5. So now you’re probably wondering, “What is a desirable rate of weight loss or gain?”</strong></h3><p><strong>Weight loss:</strong> I recommend <strong>0.5–0.75%</strong> of body weight loss <em><strong>per week</strong></em> when cutting<em>.</em> — The leaner you get, the slower you should take it. Most busy people will find anything faster than this unsustainable, due to increasing fatigue, hunger, and brain fog.</p><p><strong>Weight gain:</strong> I recommend <strong>0.5–2%</strong> of body weight gain <em><strong>per month</strong></em> when bulking<em>.</em> The more experienced a trainee you are, the closer you are to your genetic potential, so the slower you should take things.</p><ul class="wp-block-list"><li><strong>Beginner: Choose 1.5–2%</strong> (If you have no training experience and little history of regular physical activity, such as sports.)</li><li><strong>Novice: Choose 1–1.5%</strong> (If you can add load and/or reps each time you lift for most or all exercises. This phase can last years, but can be as short as 6 months with an appropriate dose, consistency, effort, recovery, and focus on progression.)</li></ul><p><strong>Intermediate: Choose 0.5–1%</strong> (If you can only add reps in the 10–20 rep range week-to-week, or for the lower rep ranges, add load month-to-month. Most never pass this phase. Can finish by year 4–5 with an appropriate dose, consistency, effort, recovery, and focus on progression.)</p><p><strong>Advanced: Choose ≤0.5%</strong> (If you can add reps in the 10–20 rep range month-to-month, or for the lower rep ranges, add reps or load at slower rates. Most never reach this phase. Progress continually slows as you get closer and closer to your potential.)</p><p>Using the upper end of the ranges will make the changes easier to track, especially when you aren’t working with a coach and are doing things yourself.</p><p><strong><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👉" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f449.svg"> More here: <a href="https://rippedbody.com/calories/" type="post" id="33623">How To Set Calories For Muscle Gain and Fat Loss</a>.</strong></p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>6. Most people will have a jump in scale weight in the first week</strong>, especially when cutting</h3><p>This will be due to the change in your body’s gut content, water, and muscle glycogen. It happens whenever you change the number of carbohydrates you eat or the total food intake in general.</p><p>So, before deciding to adjust, track for several weeks first, taking the average scale weight each day, and ignore the first week of data.</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>7. To make a macro adjustment to get your body weight change on track…</strong></h3><p><strong>If you are cutting,</strong> multiply the amount you are off your <em>weekly</em> weight change target by 500 kcal (or 1100 kcal for those using kg). Add or subtract that from your caloric intake each day accordingly. Here are some examples:</p><ul class="wp-block-list"><li>If you lose weight 0.5 lbs <em>slower</em> than your target each week, <em>reduce</em> caloric intake by 250 kcal daily (500*0.5). — This is approximately one cup of uncooked rice or 3 cups of cooked rice.</li><li>If you lose weight 0.3 lbs <em>faster</em> than your target each week, <em>increase</em> caloric intake by 150 kcal daily (0.3*500). — This is approximately a 2 oz portion of uncooked pasta.</li></ul><p> <strong><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👉" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f449.svg"> More here: <a href="https://rippedbody.com/how-to-adjust-macros/">How to Adjust Calories and Macros as You Diet</a> <img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="📉" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f4c9.svg"></strong></p><p>Make this caloric change via fat and carb changes per your preferences. This is as simple as follows:</p><figure class="wp-block-image aligncenter"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="306" src="https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Table-of-Macro-Changes-for-Different-Calorie-Adjustments-1024x306.png" alt="Macro Calculator Adjustment Table " class="wp-image-58619" srcset="https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Table-of-Macro-Changes-for-Different-Calorie-Adjustments-1024x306.png 1024w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Table-of-Macro-Changes-for-Different-Calorie-Adjustments-450x135.png 450w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Table-of-Macro-Changes-for-Different-Calorie-Adjustments-768x230.png 768w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Table-of-Macro-Changes-for-Different-Calorie-Adjustments-1568x469.png 1568w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Table-of-Macro-Changes-for-Different-Calorie-Adjustments.png 1738w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Table-of-Macro-Changes-for-Different-Calorie-Adjustments-450x135@2x.png 900w" sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px"><figcaption class="wp-element-caption">Table of Macro Changes for Different Calorie Adjustments</figcaption></figure><p><strong>If you are bulking,</strong> multiply the amount you are off your <em>monthly</em> weight change target by 150 kcal (or 330 kcal for those using kg). (These numbers are explained in my <a href="https://rippedbody.com/how-to-bulk/">mega-guide on bulking</a>). Here are some examples:</p><ul class="wp-block-list"><li>If you’re gaining weight 1.5 lb <em>slower</em> than your target each <em>month</em>, increase daily caloric intake by 225 kcal (150*1.5).</li><li>If you’re gaining weight 0.5 kg <em>faster</em> than your target each <em>month</em>, reduce daily caloric intake by ~165 kcal (330*0.5).</li></ul><p> <strong><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👉" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f449.svg"> More here:</strong> <strong><a href="https://rippedbody.com/how-to-adjust-macros-bulk/">How to Adjust Calories and Macros as You Bulk</a> <img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="📈" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f4c8.svg"></strong></p><p>Whether cutting or bulking, leave protein intake as is unless you have a lot of body fat to lose. (More on this in a moment.)</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>8. Before making any adjustments, make sure your adherence is on point</strong></h3><p>If your adherence is not on point, fix that before adjusting your macros. Solid adherence in the week, only to throw it away on the weekends, is the most common pattern people follow. This includes adherence to your <a href="https://rippedbody.com/free-programs-course/">workouts</a> — your training routine needs to be <em>routine; plans don’t mean anything if they aren’t followed through</em>.</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>9. Double-check that you are tracking things accurately</strong></h3><p><a href="https://rippedbody.com/how-to-count-macros/">Here’s my guide to counting macros and making meals out of them</a>. (The simplified counting rules in this guide are what gave the food value estimations you saw at the end of the calculator.)</p><p>But despite guides like that trying to simplify, studies consistently show that people are terrible at tracking things. So, if you’re not losing weight at the rate desired and you’re concerned that your macros look low, swallow your ego and consider the possibility that you’ve screwed something up.</p><p>Log everything that passes your lips into a nutritional calculator for 2 weeks. This will tell you if you have an issue.</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>10. The Way Protein intake is calculated Depends On A Few Factors</strong></h3><p>Many calculators set protein intake based on total body weight. However, this can lead to an unnecessarily high protein intake, especially for individuals with a significant amount of fat to lose. This makes things difficult. Past a certain point, more protein isn’t beneficial; it’s a burden. It’s more expensive and makes it more difficult to hit your calorie budget without consuming too much fat. Swapping excess protein for carbs and fat will lead to better satiety, diet variety, and training quality.</p><p>To address this issue, if you have a good estimate of your body fat percentage (which should not be taken for granted, because <a href="https://rippedbody.com/is-it-better-to-forget-about-body-fat-percentage/">all of the methods available for estimating body fat percentage are prone to large error levels</a>, we calculate protein based on <em>lean</em> body mass (body weight minus fat). If you don’t have an estimate of your body fat percentage, we use your target body weight when cutting, and your current body weight when recomping or bulking.</p><p><strong>When body fat percentage is known:</strong></p><ul class="wp-block-list"><li><strong>Bulking or Recomposition:</strong> 1 g per pound of lean mass (2.2 g per kg)</li><li><strong>Cutting:</strong> 1.14 g per pound of lean mass (2.5 g per kg)</li></ul><p><strong>When body fat percentage is unknown:</strong></p><ul class="wp-block-list"><li><strong>Bulking or Recomposition:</strong> 0.73 g per pound (1.6 g/kg) of body weight.</li><li><strong>Cutting:</strong> 1 g per pound (2.2 g/kg) of <em>target</em> body weight.</li></ul><p>You could also consider setting protein intake based on your height in cm.</p><figure class="wp-block-image aligncenter size-large is-resized"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="971" height="1024" src="https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Recommended-Protein-Intake-For-Overweight-or-Obese-People-971x1024.png" alt="Necessary protein intake changes for those considerably over weight." class="wp-image-59074" style="width:600px" srcset="https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Recommended-Protein-Intake-For-Overweight-or-Obese-People-971x1024.png 971w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Recommended-Protein-Intake-For-Overweight-or-Obese-People-427x450.png 427w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Recommended-Protein-Intake-For-Overweight-or-Obese-People-729x768.png 729w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Recommended-Protein-Intake-For-Overweight-or-Obese-People-1943x2048.png 1943w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Recommended-Protein-Intake-For-Overweight-or-Obese-People-1568x1653.png 1568w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Recommended-Protein-Intake-For-Overweight-or-Obese-People.png 2048w, aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div><p>If you’re 260 lbs and 6’0, you can clearly see how the traditional “1 g per pound of body weight” recommendation is too high.</p><p>However, if you are targeting 20 lbs of fat loss, but you know you could likely lose 50 lbs of fat, then I recommend setting your target body weight as 210 lbs, because 240 g of protein would still be too high. You can cross-check this against the graph above; you want to be in that ballpark, but not too much higher.</p><p>Using whey protein to help you meet your numbers is fine, but real food tends to be more filling. Speaking about food, don’t worry about “complete” and “incomplete” proteins — whey and animal products may be the most “complete” proteins, but as long as you hit your targets by the end of the day, it won’t make a difference.</p><p>For a fuller explanation of these protein guidelines, including why protein needs are higher when dieting, see our article: <a href="https://rippedbody.com/macros/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"><em>How to Set Your Macros for Fat Loss and Muscle Growth</em></a>.</p><p>References can be found in my book, <a href="https://muscleandstrengthpyramids.com/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener"><em>The Muscle and Strength Pyramid: Nutrition</em></a>.</p><figure class="wp-block-image aligncenter size-large is-resized"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="1024" height="1024" src="https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-1024x1024.jpg" alt="Macro Calculator - Protein, fat and carb guidelines" class="wp-image-59442" style="width:600px" srcset="https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-1024x1024.jpg 1024w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-450x450.jpg 450w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-150x150.jpg 150w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-768x768.jpg 768w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-1536x1536.jpg 1536w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-2048x2048.jpg 2048w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-1568x1568.jpg 1568w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-450x450@2x.jpg 900w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Macro-Calculator-Protein-fat-and-carb-guidelines-150x150@2x.jpg 300w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px"></figure><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h4 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>12. Too many people make calorie and macro calculations, wait two weeks, then decide that they “don’t work” if they don’t see the calculated for scale weight change</strong></h4><p>They then move to another <strong>macro calculator</strong> (or diet method entirely). This is sadly common. Don’t be this person!</p><p>A simple adjustment of your caloric intake up or downwards is all that will be needed. (Reasons 2-4, at rates described in 5, in the manner described in 7.) Don’t make this mistake.</p><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity"><h4 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>13. If you have already calculated your macros elsewhere and they aren’t radically different, I don’t suggest you change them</strong></h4><p>As I’ve covered, <em>the key is that you track your progress and adjust based on the outcome</em>. You’ll see I have several guides on that in the <a href="https://rippedbody.com/nutrition/#adjustments">diet adjustments section</a> of the <a href="https://rippedbody.com/nutrition/">nutrition articles page</a>, where you’ll find helpful decision trees like this:</p><figure class="wp-block-image aligncenter size-large"><a href="https://rippedbody.com/how-to-adjust-macros/"><img loading="lazy" decoding="async" width="405" height="1024" src="https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Diet-Adjustments-Decision-Tree-2-405x1024.png" alt="" class="wp-image-61105" srcset="https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Diet-Adjustments-Decision-Tree-2-405x1024.png 405w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Diet-Adjustments-Decision-Tree-2-178x450.png 178w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Diet-Adjustments-Decision-Tree-2-607x1536.png 607w, https://rippedbody.com/wp-content/uploads/Diet-Adjustments-Decision-Tree-2.png 760w" sizes="auto, (max-width: 405px) 100vw, 405px"></a></figure><hr class="wp-block-separator has-css-opacity is-style-wide"><h3 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-center">MACRO CALCULATOR FAQ</h3><div class="wp-block-uagb-faq uagb-faq__outer-wrap uagb-block-b37f646a uagb-faq-icon-row uagb-faq-layout-accordion uagb-faq-expand-first-false uagb-faq-inactive-other-true uagb-faq__wrap uagb-buttons-layout-wrap uagb-faq-equal-height     " data-faqtoggle="true" role="tablist"><script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https:\/\/schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","@id":"https:\/\/rippedbody.com\/macro-calculator\/","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"<strong>How do I know which activity multiplier to choose?<\/strong>","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Just choose the one you feel is most descriptive of your situation and don't worry about it. You’ll adjust your calculation in a few weeks based on the outcome anyway.</p></div></div><div class="wp-block-uagb-faq-child uagb-faq-child__outer-wrap uagb-faq-item uagb-block-5d79fdd8 " role="tab" tabindex="0"><div class="uagb-faq-questions-button uagb-faq-questions"> <span class="uagb-icon uagb-faq-icon-wrap"> <svg xmlns="https://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 448 512"><path d="M432 256c0 17.69-14.33 32.01-32 32.01H256v144c0 17.69-14.33 31.99-32 31.99s-32-14.3-32-31.99v-144H48c-17.67 0-32-14.32-32-32.01s14.33-31.99 32-31.99H192v-144c0-17.69 14.33-32.01 32-32.01s32 14.32 32 32.01v144h144C417.7 224 432 238.3 432 256z"></path></svg> </span> <span class="uagb-icon-active uagb-faq-icon-wrap"> <svg xmlns="https://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 448 512"><path d="M400 288h-352c-17.69 0-32-14.32-32-32.01s14.31-31.99 32-31.99h352c17.69 0 32 14.3 32 31.99S417.7 288 400 288z"></path></svg> </span><p class="uagb-question"><strong>Why is the total energy intake calculated the same regardless of whether I choose 3 or 6 training days?</strong></p></div><div class="uagb-faq-content"><p>I’ve built the calculator to assume that everyone trains between 3–6 days per week. The number of workouts has not been set to affect the calorie needs calculation because in total weekly energy demands for 3 vs 6 workouts per week is small enough to ignore.(You might burn 300 kcal in the average resistance training workout, so an additional training day adds only ~43 kcal to daily energy needs.)In the latter step, where I give people the option for calorie and macro cycling, this just divides the calories and macros across the week based on the number of workouts. There aren’t more calories overall.</p></div></div><div class="wp-block-uagb-faq-child uagb-faq-child__outer-wrap uagb-faq-item uagb-block-e7000c14 wp-block-uagb-faq-child uagb-faq-child__outer-wrap uagb-faq-item uagb-block-e7000c14" role="tab" tabindex="0"><div class="uagb-faq-questions-button uagb-faq-questions"> <span class="uagb-icon uagb-faq-icon-wrap"> <svg xmlns="https://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 448 512"><path d="M432 256c0 17.69-14.33 32.01-32 32.01H256v144c0 17.69-14.33 31.99-32 31.99s-32-14.3-32-31.99v-144H48c-17.67 0-32-14.32-32-32.01s14.33-31.99 32-31.99H192v-144c0-17.69 14.33-32.01 32-32.01s32 14.32 32 32.01v144h144C417.7 224 432 238.3 432 256z"></path></svg> </span> <span class="uagb-icon-active uagb-faq-icon-wrap"> <svg xmlns="https://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 448 512"><path d="M400 288h-352c-17.69 0-32-14.32-32-32.01s14.31-31.99 32-31.99h352c17.69 0 32 14.3 32 31.99S417.7 288 400 288z"></path></svg> </span><p class="uagb-question"><strong>Why does the calorie intake drop each day when I select more training days and cycle them?</strong></p></div><div class="uagb-faq-content"><p>This answer builds on the previous one.The option for macro cycling divides the <em>same</em> calories and macros across the week based on the number of workouts. The training days are set to have a higher calorie intake than the rest days. Therefore, when there are more training days, the calorie number for each day must go down to maintain the balance. <em>There aren’t fewer calories overall</em>.</p></div></div></div><div style="height:60px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div><div class="convertkit-form wp-block-convertkit-form" style=""><form action="https://app.kit.com/forms/7749683/subscriptions" style="background-color:#fffefa;border-radius:10px" class="seva-form formkit-form" method="post" data-sv-form="7749683" data-uid="8462769b72" data-format="inline" data-version="5" min-width="400 500 600 700"><div data-style="full" style="--bg-border-radius:5px"><div data-element="column" class="formkit-column"><div class="formkit-header" style="color:#333333;font-weight:700" data-element="header"><h2><strong>STOP GUESSING. itemprop="name">Andy Morgan</h4> </a><div class="post-author-desc">Hi! The Ripped Body® Method is an evidence-based, data-driven approach to physique transformation.
⠀
For the majority of my 20s, I fell for the scams of the fitness industry. <img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="🤦🏻‍♂️" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f926-1f3fb-200d-2642-fe0f.svg">
⠀
This site is my revenge. <img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="😬" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f62c.svg">
⠀
Since 2011, I’ve written guides explaining how we get results to help readers avoid the same path of frustration. We’ve helped over 2,000 busy working guys transform their bodies WITHOUT supplements, fad diets, or the process dominating their lives.
⠀
<img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="📩" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f4e9.svg"> Enter your email address in the box at the bottom of this page and I'd be honored to show you how.</div></div></div></div></div><div class="wpdiscuz_top_clearing"></div><div id="comments" class="comments-area"><div id="respond" style="width: 0;height: 0;clear: both;margin: 0;padding: 0;"></div><div id="wpdcom" class="wpdiscuz_unauth wpd-default wpd-layout-1 wpd-comments-open"><div class="wc_social_plugin_wrapper"></div><div class="wpd-form-wrap"><div class="wpd-form-head"><div class="wpd-auth"><div class="wpd-login"></div></div></div><div class="wpd-form wpd-form-wrapper wpd-main-form-wrapper" id="wpd-main-form-wrapper-0_0"><form method="post" enctype="multipart/form-data" data-uploading="false" class="wpd_comm_form wpd_main_comm_form"><p style="font-size:20px; font-weight:900; text-transform:uppercase; margin-bottom:10px; margin-top:40px;">Got a question?     <div class="wpd-comment-text">
    <p>Hello -for the past 150 days I have been on a cut dropping from 88kg to 79kg. However as of yesterday I have broken a bone in my foot and am going to be out action for weeks. I have been on a split pattern of eating / training (4days). I was targeting another 5kg. What should I do know? Eat at maintenance or slightly lower ? I not going to be able to go to the gym with a boot on my foot.</p>

</div>

    <div class="wpd-comment-text">
    <p>Hi RAK, sorry to hear that.</p>
<p>Dieting without strength training risks losing muscle mass. So, I would recommend <a href="https://rippedbody.com/finding-calorie-maintenance/" target="_blank" rel="ugc">returning to maintenance calorie intake</a> until you are able to resume.</p>
<p>Wishing you a speedy recovery!</p>

    <div class="wpd-comment-date" title="May 13, 2026 04:37">
    <i class="far fa-clock" aria-hidden="true"></i>

    <div class="wpd-space"></div>
    <div class="wpd-comment-link wpd-hidden">
    <span wpd-tooltip="Comment Link" wpd-tooltip-position="left"><i class="fas fa-link" aria-hidden="true" data-wpd-clipboard="https://rippedbody.com/macro-calculator/#comment-92606"></i></span>

    <div class="wpd-reply-to">
    <i class="far fa-comments"></i>
    Reply to 
    <a href="#comment-92604">
        JohnInCA
    </a>
</div>
    <div class="wpd-comment-text">
    <p>Hi John, thank you for the question.</p>
<p>First, congrats on the successful cut.</p>
<p>~3000 isn’t unusual, but we don’t have to guess at this. We can do some math.</p>
<p>You’ll see in point two that I don’t recommend using calculators like this when transitioning from a cut to bulk or maintenance, or vice-versa. This is because calculators only provide estimations, but you when you already have your own tracking data, it’s more accurate to adjust based on that.</p>
<p>See this article: <a href="https://rippedbody.com/finding-calorie-maintenance/" rel="ugc">How to Find Maintenance Calories After Dieting</a></p>
<p>You’ll see this calculation there:</p>
<p><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👉🏻" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f449-1f3fb.svg"> <em>Adjustment to find maintenance ≈ average weekly weight change in pounds x 500 + weight in pounds</em> </p>
<p>If your experience was typical, weight loss was faster in the opening weeks. This was due to losses of water, gut content, and glycogen (WGG), which you’ll regain when you return to maintenance. That might be ~5 lbs. So, if your recent average weekly weight of weight loss has been 1 lb per week, then you’ll add 500 + 172 kcal to your current intake, which brings you to 2972 kcal.</p>
<p>This is fairly close to what the calculator happens to be estimating.</p>
<p>Feel free to bump calories by a lower amount if you really wish, but know that a regain in weight initially is to be expected (WGG) so you can’t judge whether you are in a surplus or not for a few weeks. For this reason, I’d make the jump in one go, right now.</p>

</div>
